items.title: SA government plans to remove 10-year fracking ban in state's South East

children.content: The South Australian government is planning to lift a ban on fracking in the state's South East amid reports of increasing risks to gas supply in southern parts of the nation.

gallery.altText: Peter Malinauskas stand in front of a SA government banner

thumbnail.altText: Peter Malinauskas stand in front of a SA government banner

items.title: Fire at North Adelaide Golf Course suspicious, police say

children.content: Police say a fire destroyed a temporary structure often used at building sites and are calling for any witnesses who saw suspicious activity to come forward.

image.altText: A temporary building at a building site gutted by fire

items.title: Gun shop owners grapple with uncertainty amid firearm reform

children.content: A lack of clarity on the South Australian government's position on gun control reform has caused a downturn in revenue for retailers.

image.altText: A man stands at a shop counter, guns are behind him

items.title: Protesters demand 'stop the chop' at rally opposing North Adelaide golf project

children.content: More than 2,000 people have gathered at Parliament House in Adelaide to protest against the chopping down of trees to make way for the North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment.

gallery.altText: Hundreds of protesters gather on the steps of South Australia's parliament.
Hundreds of protesters gather on the steps of South Australia's parliament.
A person holds a chainsaw up to a branch of a tree

thumbnail.altText: Hundreds of protesters gather on the steps of South Australia's parliament.
Hundreds of protesters gather on the steps of South Australia's parliament.

items.title: Notorious paedophile's lawyer says indefinite jail would be an 'abuse of process'

children.content: A lawyer for paedophile former magistrate Peter Liddy asks a court to permanently stay an application to keep his client behind bars, but a lawyer for the SA Attorney-General says Liddy continues to deny his crimes.

image.altText: Peter Liddy

items.title: Whyalla steelworks remains 'fiscal risk' in budget as SA waits for freight bypass funding

children.content: The federal government's ongoing financial support for the Whyalla steelworks remains a "fiscal risk" to the budget. Meanwhile, the South Australian government continues to hold out for more Commonwealth funding for a major road project.

image.altText: A view from near the blast furnace of the Whyalla steelworks.

items.title: Omega-3 testing to be introduced in SA to prevent preterm birth

children.content: The South Australian government has announced that pregnant women will be able to access free omega-3 blood tests from next month.

image.altText: A mother holds her baby in a lab

items.title: No evidence found on first day of new search for SA outback mother

children.content: SA Police say searches in the outback town of Andamooka on Wednesday failed to recover any evidence relating to the disappearance of missing local woman Trisha Graf.

image.altText: Two people sit in the entrance to a large hole with dirt around and a four-wheel drive

items.title: Woman stole parrot from pet shop by hiding it under top, police allege

children.content: South Australian police swoop on the alleged perpetrator of a parrot theft at a suburban Adelaide pet shop, but say the bird has not been found.

image.altText: A parrot police say was stolen.

items.title: Midnight Oil's Peter Garrett 'closes the circle' to chair Landcare

children.content: The former frontman for the Australian rockers was among those who helped lobby for the formation of Landcare Australia in 1989, so it seems fitting he should circle back to chair the organisation after a lifetime lobbying for the environment.

image.altText: man wearing hat stands beside tree

items.title: Duo swindled $6 million from five victims in investment scam, court hears

children.content: Two men fleeced millions from five victims in a fraudulent property investment scheme, which one victim said had stolen half her superannuation and "ruined" her life.

gallery.altText: Bare branches covering a large beige building in the background

thumbnail.altText: Bare branches covering a large beige building in the background

avatar.alt: A woman with blonde hair wearing a black blazer and white top

items.title: SA man forced to spend night in hospital ED due to lack of access cabs

children.content: A multiple sclerosis patient says he waited three hours for an access cab, only to be told in the middle of the night one would not be available, forcing him to spend the night at an Adelaide emergency room.

image.altText: A man sits in a mobility wheelchair.

items.title: Adelaide University students critical after global data breach

children.content: Adelaide University says access to the cloud-based learning platform Canvas is back online after a data breach last week, but some students have criticised the institution's communication about the incident.

image.altText: Two young men with dark curly hair stand side by side surrounded by greenery on university grounds.

items.title: Sexual abuse, a thwarted Olympic dream, and the sister who uncovered the truth

children.content: Ian Walker was one of the most promising athletes in the country until his life started to unravel, and the search for the truth in his tragic story has finally come to light.

gallery.altText: An older image of three young siblings arm in arm smiling at each other.
A young man wearing black pants and a blue jumper stands smiling beneath a poster of Jim Morrison.

thumbnail.altText: An older image of three young siblings arm in arm smiling at each other.
A young man wearing black pants and a blue jumper stands smiling beneath a poster of Jim Morrison.

items.title: King opens UK parliament but doomed PM Starmer may not last the week

children.content: Sir Keir Starmer's future as Britain's prime minister remains on life support amid mounting speculation he could face a leadership challenge as early as Thursday, local time.

image.altText: A man in a suit looking on

items.title: Australians 'literally dying' while they wait months for aged care packages

children.content: Opposition MPs accuse the federal government of burying figures showing Australians are waiting a year for residential aged care and at-home support packages.

image.altText: A bed in a resident's room in an aged care home.

items.title: Economists divided on extent of property price hit from budget tax overhaul

children.content: Some economists are forecasting price falls of up to 5 per cent as a result of measures to reduce intergenerational housing inequality.

articles.title: 'Won’t be good returns': Landlords divided on property investment after budget

children.content: Housing policy changes announced in the federal budget could make it easier for some to buy homes, but others are planning to sell before they lose too much.

image.altText: A woman sits with her legs crossed on a stone wall overlooking the ocean.

articles.title: Angus Taylor proposes one-for-one migration cap tied to housing

children.content: The Coalition wants to limit Australia's net migration intake to only one person per new home built, but would wait until in government to decide what visa types to slash if elected.

image.altText: A man in a suit extends his hand in exclamation while speaking to a group of journalists.

items.title: Coles found guilty of misleading customers in fake discounts case

children.content: Watch the verdict coming down in the ACCC's case against a major supermarket. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: An ASX board shot by John Gunn in Deccember 2022.

items.title: Alex Murdaugh murder convictions overturned

children.content: An infamous case will be retried after a US court ruled in favour of the South Carolina father.

image.altText: a man in orange jumpsuit and glasses clasps hands listening in court

items.title: Residents fear houses could fall into the ocean and nothing is being done

children.content: Residents living on an idyllic stretch of the NSW far south coast are demanding action to protect their homes from erosion.

gallery.altText: storm debris at the back of a house

thumbnail.altText: storm debris at the back of a house

items.title: Almost 900 workers evacuated after chemical spill at meat processing plant

children.content: Five people were hospitalised after the liquid ammonia spill occurred just after midnight at a meat processing facility in Ipswich.

image.altText: Emergency crews respond to a chemical spill at a meat processing plant in Ipswich, May 14 2026.

items.title: Child sex offender resigns as Melbourne Karen Buddhist group president

children.content: A convicted child sex offender who was told he was exempt from requiring a Working With Children Check has resigned as the president of a community organisation.

image.altText: An elderly man wearing a green jacket speaking into a microphone.

items.title: Is the devil in the detail when Anne Hathaway goes toe-to-toe with Michaela Coel?

children.content: Director David Lowery has crafted a dark and mysterious chamber piece that turns a pop star's spiralling crisis into something supernatural.

items.title: Which country has won Eurovision the most? Take our More or Less quiz

children.content: We've put down our tiny flags and picked up our record books to challenge what you think you might know about Eurovision.

image.altText: Kate Miller-Heidke is reaching towards the camera, suspended on a pole, with a microphone in opposite hand.

items.title: Blockbuster movies are connecting new audiences to classical music

children.content: From 19th-century concert traditions to Hollywood’s Golden Age, and back to the concert hall, movies are helping audiences experience live orchestral music.

image.altText: animation of young boy with brown hair with black dragon

items.title: 'Portrait of anger' by Australian cartoonist wins $60,000 Stella Prize

children.content: Lee Lai's debut graphic novel, Stone Fruit, was longlisted for the Stella Prize in 2022. Four years on, she's won the prestigious award for her latest book, Cannon.

gallery.altText: A close-up photo of a person with short dark hair and glasses, smiling and wearing a tan checked shirt.

thumbnail.altText: A close-up photo of a person with short dark hair and glasses, smiling and wearing a tan checked shirt.

items.title: Sydney real estate agent fined $35k for falsely evicting tenant

children.content: One Sydney real estate agent was been fined $35,000 for evicting a tenant on the grounds that the landlord's relative wanted to live in the property, only they never moved in.

image.altText: A black and white for lease sign woth man in front

items.title: Canvas developers could be added to 'sucker list' after deal with hackers

children.content: The company that runs Canvas has painted a target on its back for future extortion attempts by making a deal with hackers, according to cybersecurity experts.

image.altText: a man sitting at a computer

items.title: Public memorial for James Valentine to be held in Sydney

children.content: Sydney Town Hall will host a memorial for the late radio presenter and musician, with limited spots available for members of the public.

image.altText: a man smiles while holding his chin

items.title: Former Wallabies coach handed ban and pay cut for abusing officials

children.content: Eddie Jones has been suspended from coaching Japan during its rugby union Test match against Italy in July after "incidences of verbal abuse" during a recent tour of Australia.

image.altText: Man in a hooded top standing in an empty stadium, looking skyward

items.title: Shots fired in Philippine senate as ICC seeks to arrest ex-police chief

children.content: Authorities are still trying to work out who fired the gun shots inside the senate building in the Philippines, prompting people to run for cover.
